user_query:
I’m coordinating the Assam INC Youth team’s recent voter outreach event and need a clean, locally verifiable finance reconciliation and communication pack built from the data in input/. Please do the following using only the provided files and write all outputs under the paths specified:

Inputs to use (all are required):
- input/expenses.csv
- input/donations.csv
- input/budget.csv
- input/team_contacts.csv

Analysis and deliverables:
1) Create outputs/category_variance.csv with one row per category and the following columns: category, budget_allocated (INR), actual_spend (INR, include both paid and unpaid reimbursements), variance_amount (actual_spend - budget_allocated), variance_pct (variance_amount / budget_allocated as a percentage; if budget is zero, set 0), over_budget (true/false), flagged_over_5pct (true if variance_pct > 5% and variance_amount > 0, else false).

2) Produce outputs/financial_summary.md with clear sections containing at least:
   - Totals:
     • Total donations received (INR)
     • Total pledges not yet received (INR)
     • Total expenses paid (INR)
     • Total pending reimbursements (INR; sum of reimbursable where reimbursement_status = unpaid)
     • Net cash position (INR; received − paid)
     • Additional funds needed to cover all expenses including pending reimbursements (INR; max(0, total_actual_spend − total_received))
   - Per-Category Budget vs Actual Summary: a brief narrative referencing outputs/category_variance.csv.
   - Over-Budget Categories (>5% over budget): list category names with variance_pct.
   - Top 3 donors by amount received (name and amount; ignore pledges for the ranking).
   Note: Prefix currency values with “INR ”.

3) Prepare outputs/meeting_notes.md with action items for the next youth committee meeting. Base all items on the data and include at least:
   - Follow-up on pledged donors: list each pledged donor (name and amount).
   - Process reimbursements: list each unpaid reimbursement with payee name and amount.
   - Discuss over-budget categories (>5%): list category name(s) and variance summary.
   - Donor appreciation assignments: assign the highest-amount donor to “Rohit Das” and the next two highest donors to “Anisha Paul” for thank-you calls/messages.
   - Fundraising plan: propose the immediate target equal to the computed “Additional funds needed to cover all expenses including pending reimbursements”.

4) Draft outputs/email_treasurer.txt addressed using team_contacts:
   - To: the Treasurer’s email from team_contacts.csv; CC: the Convener’s email.
   - Include a subject line and a concise body that:
     • States Total donations received, Total expenses paid, Total pending reimbursements (with each unpaid reimbursement listed by payee and amount), and Net cash position.
     • Names the over-budget categories (>5%) and their variance_pct.
     • Includes a clear ask to approve pending reimbursements and to review the over-budget items.
   Use INR-prefixed amounts.

5) Draft a short outputs/message_volunteers.txt for sharing via WhatsApp/SMS to the volunteer group:
   - Include: greeting to the team, Net cash position, Pledges not yet received, Top 3 donors (names and amounts), and the immediate collection target equal to the “Additional funds needed to cover all expenses including pending reimbursements”.
   - End with two next steps for volunteers: (1) follow up on listed pledges, (2) coordinate reimbursements with the Treasurer.

Definitions to use consistently:
- Actual spend for category comparisons = all expenses in that category including unpaid reimbursements.
- Net cash position = Total donations received − Total expenses paid (exclude unpaid reimbursements from “paid”).
- Additional funds needed = max(0, Total actual spend (including unpaid reimbursements) − Total donations received).

Please ensure all four output files are created exactly at:
- outputs/category_variance.csv
- outputs/financial_summary.md
- outputs/meeting_notes.md
- outputs/email_treasurer.txt
- outputs/message_volunteers.txt

Use only the provided inputs and make no assumptions beyond the data. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
